original text:
Where did the year go!? The last few months of 2014 feel like a blur of footy finals, Thanksgiving, and bad facial hair. And companies hiring. Always hiring.
2014 was a massive year for RecruitLoop, in our journey to transform recruiting and make hiring easy. I want to say a heartfelt thanks to everyone who played a part in it: our Clients, Community, investors, advocates. And especially to our team (and their families), who worked tirelessly throughout the year to make it all possible.
Before we launch into the new year, a quick pause to reflect and celebrate 2014, in the rearview.
Team of 10 people across 4 countries, with 20,039 messages posted to our internal chat rooms (most of them automated!).
2 new offices, and 4 new office pets.
We're on a mission to bring transparency and fairness to the process (and industry) of recruiting - for everyone involved. We expanded our platform in 2014, and launched some exciting new features to do exactly that.
In the outside world, finding and identifying the best recruiter for a specific role is an almost impossible task. We're solving that problem with detailed feedback and ratings. Now, every recruiter is rated on several criteria after every project: professionalism, responsiveness, efficiency, expertise and value. Clients can also offer public (and private) comments for review.
As consumers, we're starting to expect this type of transparency in almost every other profession. But it's unheard of for recruiters. You'll be seeing these ratings on new and improved Recruiter Profiles in early 2015.
We carefully vet the experience of every verified recruiter in our Community. A big part of this is references from a recruiter's previous clients and candidates. Now, when we ask a recruiter for a reference, that referee must verify their comments using their LinkedIn credentials. This ensures the references you see on a recruiter's profile are from real people, who stand behind them.
Our clients demand transparency about potential Recruiters the might work with. One way we delivered that in 2014 was with a simple, cleaner view of Proposals. Now, clients can view any recruiters' proposal with a single click, from any device (no login required!). They can ask a question, accept or decline each proposal in a matter of seconds.
A big internal development in 2014 focused on our marketplace management platform. This allows (amongst many things) much faster, more targeted matching between clients and recruiters. Now, when a clients posts a role, they receive only 2-3 proposals from recruiters with specific, relevant experience - often within minutes. There's no fancy screenshots of this one. The only way to try it is to post a role.
In the outside world: on sites like Inc., OnStartups, Entrpreneur.com.
What is Market Mapping and How Do You Do It?
Infographics: The True Cost of Hiring.
Our Community is the lifeblood of RecruitLoop. It grew and thrived in 2014 - sometimes of our own making, but increasingly (and excitingly) with a life of its own.
Tamara Blythman - who won a trip from Sydney to San Francisco!
Kim Acworth - who redeemed her points for sponsorship of her rally car.
Christie Heiner - who redeemed her points for one year of LinkedIn Recruiter membership.
We hosted Community over a dozen meetups (physical and virtual) throughout 2014. Some were purely social, but brought people together. Our professional development series also had great feedback, with topics like How to be a Legendary Looper; and Maximize your Consultant Mojo.
It wasn't all a year of smiles and roses. It was difficult in many ways. Like any (every?) startup, we've made mistakes and learnt something new every day. As we grow - and grow up - as a company, I hope we're constantly improving, based on those sometimes painful lessons.
Doing too many ‘things that don't scale'.
We followed the famous advice. We tried to address broken processes with human input. We carefully curated every element of our marketplace to provide a phenomenal service to everyone involved. But we reached a point late in the year where this accumulation of small decisions - despite the best of intentions - was actually making it harder for us scale. This was a difficult realization; and we're now in the process of streamlining and automating many parts of the business, to ensure we have a truly scalable model.
Some people don't like change.
Actually, tons of people don't like change. This is an obvious statement, and something every new innovation needs to overcome. But it's a sentiment we felt in a few different ways this year: in a strategic partnership that flopped soon after takeoff; in another that got close, but fell just before the finish line; in some poorly disguised hacks and critiques from disgruntled traditionalists.
Some of these lessons hurt in the moment, but have made us stronger on reflection. We're unmoved in our resolve to change the status quo; and now in a better position to do it with another year of experience behind us.
An Expanded Community. We're making some major changes to our platform and guidelines, to expand the reach and scope of our Community. Expect to see different categories of recruiters and Talent Professionals, based on their level of skills, experience, and verification.
New and Improved Profiles. We'll be rolling out new designs for Community Profiles, to capture the depth of specialization and experience, and display detailed performance metrics and ratings.
New / improved UI. In general, we'll be progressively deploying a dramatically improved, mobile-responsive user-experience for both sides of our marketplace over the coming months.
...Along with a few more I can't share just now.
It was a fantastic year. Thank you to everyone who played a role. We're looking forward to serving you with even bigger and better changes throughout the year ahead.

original text:
" Cefazolin for Injection contains an amount of Cefazolin Sodium equivalent to not less than 90.0 percent and not more than 115.0 percent of the labeled amount of cefazolin (C14H14N8O4S3).
Packaging and storage Preserve in Containers for Injections as described under Injections 1 .
Medium: 0.1 M sodium bicarbonate.
B: The retention time of the major peak for cefazolin in the chromatogram of the Assay preparation corresponds to that in the chromatogram of the Standard preparation, as obtained in the Assay.
Specific rotation 781S : between 10 and 24 .
Test solution: 55 mg per mL, in 0.
Bacterial endotoxins 85  It contains not more than 0.15 USP Endotoxin Unit per mg of cefazolin.
pH 791 : between 4.0 and 6.0, in a solution containing 100 mg of cefazolin per mL.
pH 3.6 Buffer, pH 7.0 Buffer, Mobile phase, Internal standard solution, Standard preparation, and Chromatographic system Prepare as directed in the Assay under Cefazolin.
Assay preparation 1 (where it is packaged for dispensing and is represented as being in a single-dose container)Constitute Cefazolin for Injection in a volume of water, accurately measured, corresponding to the volume of solvent specified in the labeling. Withdraw all of the withdrawable contents, using a suitable hypodermic needle and syringe, and dilute quantitatively with pH 7.0 buffer to obtain a stock solution containing about 1 mg of cefazolin per mL. Transfer 5.0 mL of this solution to a 100-mL volumetric flask, add 5.0 mL of Internal standard solution, dilute with pH 7.0 buffer to volume, and mix.
Assay preparation 2 (where the label states the quantity of cefazolin in a given volume of constituted solution)Constitute Cefazolin for Injection in a volume of water, accurately measured, corresponding to the volume of solvent specified in the labeling. Dilute an accurately measured volume of the constituted solution quantitatively with pH 7.
in which L is the labeled quantity, in mg, of cefazolin in the container, or in the volume of constituted solution taken; D is the concentration, in mg per mL, of cefazolin in the stock solution used in preparing Assay preparation 1 or Assay preparation 2, on the basis of the labeled quantity in the container, or in the volume of constituted solution taken, respectively, and the extent of dilution; and the other terms are as defined therein. Where the test for Uniformity of dosage units has been performed using the Procedure for content uniformity, use the average of these determinations as the Assay value.

original text:
Originally designed in 1932, this stunning lounge chair designed by Alvar Aalto dates from the 1950's. It is well documented in many images showing his finished architectural designs.
Steam bent Birch ply cantilevers and carries the upholstered seat; the high-back model was designed for comfort and relaxing, providing support for the back and neck.
A fantastic example of mid-Century design. Historical and comfortable!
Tags: 1950's, Chair, Mid-Century, Model 401, Vintage, Alvar Aalto.
In good condition with a few marks commensurate with use and age - over 60 years' old.

original text:
We at Birchwood Landscaping LLC love creating and designing landscapes that suit your wants and desires. We use photo realistic design programs to help you visualize the finished project. We walk around your home with you and lay out the project for you using any method necessary to help you understand the scope and layout of the project.
Our team of landscape installers cares about doing the job right. We care because the owners are working with their own hands on your project. Our installers are the same people who designed and worked with you on putting together the landscape plans for your outdoor space.
When you contact our staff at Birchwood Landscaping LLC, what you get is a team of dedicated professionals working with you from start to finish and then beyond. We aren't going anywhere and we will always be a phone call away.
One way we can continue to offer quality landscape workmanship is keeping our equipment well maintained. Our equipment is what gets the job done for your project, so we make sure we maintain our equipment regularly so it is just as reliable as the day we bought it.
We also welcome you to visit our nursery! We use a wide variety of plant material coming from many local nurseries that provide quality plants grown by handpicked professionals in the industry. Our landscapes are only as good as the plants we install, so we provide the best material in the business.
Remember, when you are looking for a landscaper in the western Michigan area, call the best at Birchwood Landscaping LLC!
Our landscapers - Our team is completely dedicated to giving each customer personal attention for their landscaping project and beyond.
Our equipment - We maintain our fleet to keep up with the many landscaping projects we perform.
Our nursery - Fantastic plants grown by experts. You deserve the best, so we only select the best.
Providing new installations and renovations to existing landscaping. Installing bed areas, planting trees and building rock walls... anything that touches your outdoor space. We are experts in lawncare and tree installation.
Build natural boulder walls, block walls, timber walls... concrete poured wall.
Installing paver patios, paver walls, paver posts, sidewalks... steppers through the landscape bed.
Installing underground sprinkling systems and sprinklers for residential homes.
Installing beautiful fountains is a great way to improve overall feel of your landscape.
We will act as the contract manager for your entire project... from design to clean up. We are very experienced in property management.
Including brush clearing for cleaning up that area on your property that got ‘out of hand'. Our equipment can grade your existing driveway or install a new turn around! We can handle installing and grading topsoil for a new lawn or regrade your existing bumpy lawn. We enjoy doing small excavating projects, putting in a vegetable garden? Filling in that low spot? Installing drain tile? Land Clearing Projects. Custom wood splitting projects.
Delivering small loads of topsoil, stone, manure and bark. Need to hire a smaller delivery truck? Give us a call!
We at Birchwood Landscaping love creating and designing landscapes that suit your wants and desires. We use photo realistic programs to help you visualize the finished project. We walk around your home with you and lay out the project for using any method necessary to help understand the scope and layout of your project. We specialize in landscape design, and are available for contractor opportunities in and around .
Installing landscape lighting to extend your landscape into the night. Also providing Security to that dark corner outside. All systems are low voltage lighting using the new LED technology to extend the light life and using a lot less energy.

original text:
Jewel Samad/Agence France-Presse  -  Getty Images Melody C. Barnes preparing to play golf with President Obama on Sunday in Fort Belvoir, Va.
Melody C. Barnes, a White House aide, broke through President Obama's green ceiling Sunday afternoon.
Ms. Barnes, the president's chief domestic policy adviser, became the first woman to play a round of golf with Mr. Obama since he tee'd off his presidency in January. The milestone is noteworthy as Mr. Obama has come under criticism recently for hosting a basketball game earlier this month for members of his cabinet and congress that included no women ("Bunk," Mr. Obama told NBC in an interview last week when asked about the controversy over the estrogen-free game).
Shortly after 1 p.m. on a crisp and sunny fall afternoon, Mr. Obama and Ms. Barnes took to the links at the U.S Army's Fort Belvoir in Virginia. The president wore a black short-sleeve shirt while Ms. Barnes donned a baseball cap and a dark long-sleeve shirt, according to a White House pool report prepared by Lynn Sweet of the Chicago Sun-Times.
It was not immediately known what Mr. Barnes shot on Sunday.
This story is "Bunk"! Having a slow news day, New York Times?
When it came to having women in the inner circle, Obama talks a good game but W walked it where O merely talks it.
Who cares? I like to play golf with the guys just to be a guy... talk sports, have a beer, tell off-color jokes. The President (any president) has to spend all day being PC and inclusive with the pressure of providing for a nation of people, fighting two wars, and dealing with global crises. Let the guy play some basketball with the guys or pick his Sunday foursome with whomever he wants. Yeesh... this is NOT news.
I thought the Silly Season ended on Labor Day.
I hope she didn't "let him" win!
I think Obama has long since proved his bona fides as a strong feminist.
But does the absurd media coverage the all-male basketball game has received mean that every time Obama moves a muscle, it wlll be scrutinized for politically correctness according to extreme feminists???
Well, I am certainly confident that anyone who considers the all-male basketball game to be serious evidence of Obama's sexism would never condone a bunch of women getting together without a male present .
What is this, cable? I read the Times to get away from this silliness.
Seriously? Who cares who he plays golf with? Who cares what kind of clothes he or his guest was wearing? You would think the New York Times would have more to report on with two wars going on and Obama's rating in the toilet.
I've always been a tomboy...sports fan, more comfortable hanging with the guys. I totally understand the bond amongst men, often centered around beer, sports, cigars (i.e. fun stuff!). It's a wonderful thing, just like the unique bond amongst women. The problem arises when work and important decisions are made during these all-male activities, whether intentional or not. I don't know what to say ladies...take up golf I suppose.
Unbelievable. This is "political correctness" without common sense. Dangerous.
I am ok if there are baby showers were just women are invited, I am ok if there are sports team only for girls, I am ok if there are bathrooms only for women, and I am ok if the President chooses to play sports with men. People can read way too much into things, and forget context, common sense, perspective and also a bit of humor.
Honestly, if the original story had not been on the front page, I would have thought it was a story from The Onion. I guess when the media is on a quest to find fault with someone, all areas are open.
Shouldn't Obama first decide about troops in Afghainstan before he takes time off to play golf?
my father made me take golf lessons during junior high school and high school - he said it was for my future. he was right... once the office found out that i played golf, i was invited to play with the "guys." it's true... a lot of work issues are discussed during a game - i don't think it's intentional but it happens.
Yeah, John Drew, just like it's a lot easier to be a right wing Christian if you don't apply all those silly principles to your own life-you know, the ones about feeding the poor, loving your neighbor, and all that nonesense.
Oh, puh-leeeze. Is there really nothing else to talk about?
Many times men are establishing relationships that further their careers when they get togther in activites such as these. Decisions are often made in such settings. If the all male club is sustained, women are put at a disadvantage. The people invited to such events are often the real inner circle. When women are excluded, they are put at a disadvantage.
If HRC had been elected president, how would men like it if she only invited women to all social functions and shut out men? My question is hypothetical since HRC did not win, but it would be interesting to see men's reactions to such a scenario.
For all of Obama's talk, I do think he harbors sexist attitudes.

In response to Liz: If you are playing golf with people who are talking about work you aren't really playing golf.
First of all, obviously the president and his staff seems to have realized that this is a problem. Why else, all of a sudden, Melody Barnes is playing golf with him?
Second, to all those people dismissing this story: it's not "just" a basketball game. Things like these might seem harmless on the surface, but can actually have a deleterious effect on women's upward movement in the workplace. It is exactly these kinds of informal, male networks that can give men the edge, when it comes time for performance reviews, bonuses, allocation of juicy assignments, promotions, etc.
This doesn't mean that men aren't allowed to play basketball anymore. It just means that corporate bosses need to be a lot of more sensitive about organizing after-work activities that are inclusive of everyone. If that means that you don't play basketball with your office, but with an external community league, so be it. It won't be the end of the world. Being an egalitarian leader means having to make some sacrifices.
We women who golf learned a long time ago that it helps solidify relationships, make men more comfortable off the course, and allows boht players to witness outside the office what we should already know from inside the office - namely, how people behave when things go your way - and when they don't. Is it with honesty, humility, humor, and strength? It's a great litmus test for any relationship, professional and personal. Good for President Obama for opening up the game.
What Obama organized is a text book example of a boys-only club.
The president is never "off duty," particularly when interacting with his employees.
Every moment of the president's day is scheduled. An opportunity to spend time with him is precious to anyone who wants to have an influence on public policy. That's why some people spend tens - if not hundreds - of thousands of dollars to play a round with him. This is Politics 101.
Every nay-saying commenter here acknowledged that these games help the president bond with his staff and other advisors. Bonding with your boss is important. Women deserve an equal opportunity to do this.
If you don't think its a problem that, after 22 golf games and numerous basketball games, NO women - until now - were given an equal opportunity to get face time and bond with the president, you are both a sexist and an ignoramus.
And don't fool yourselves - if a president Hillary Clinton organized 22+ golf games and a dozen bridge games and didn't invite any male staff, you male reporters and pundits and commenters would be shrieking with outrage. You'd be crying about man-hating and calling for her impeachment. Admit it.
We do NOT need a White House modeled on Mad Men. If that's the change B. has in mind, he can stuff it.
processed_text:
Jewel Samad/Agence France-Presse  -  Getty Images Melody C. Barnes preparing to play golf with President Obama on Sunday in Fort Belvoir, Va.
Melody C. Barnes, a White House aide, broke through President Obama's green ceiling Sunday afternoon.
Ms. Barnes, the president's chief domestic policy adviser, became the first woman to play a round of golf with Mr. Obama since he tee'd off his presidency in January. The milestone is noteworthy as Mr. Obama has come under criticism recently for hosting a basketball game earlier this month for members of his cabinet and congress that included no women ("Bunk," Mr. Obama told NBC in an interview last week when asked about the controversy over the estrogen-free game).
Shortly after 1 p.m. on a crisp and sunny fall afternoon, Mr. Obama and Ms. Barnes took to the links at the U.S Army's Fort Belvoir in Virginia. The president wore a black short-sleeve shirt while Ms. Barnes donned a baseball cap and a dark long-sleeve shirt, according to a White House pool report prepared by Lynn Sweet of the Chicago Sun-Times.
It was not immediately known what Mr. Barnes shot on Sunday.
This story is "Bunk"! Having a slow news day, New York Times?
When it came to having women in the inner circle, Obama talks a good game but W walked it where O merely talks it.
Who cares? I like to play golf with the guys just to be a guy... talk sports, have a beer, tell off-color jokes. The President (any president) has to spend all day being PC and inclusive with the pressure of providing for a nation of people, fighting two wars, and dealing with global crises. Let the guy play some basketball with the guys or pick his Sunday foursome with whomever he wants. Yeesh... this is NOT news.
I thought the Silly Season ended on Labor Day.
I hope she didn't "let him" win!
I think Obama has long since proved his bona fides as a strong feminist.
But does the absurd media coverage the all-male basketball game has received mean that every time Obama moves a muscle, it wlll be scrutinized for politically correctness according to extreme feminists???
Well, I am certainly confident that anyone who considers the all-male basketball game to be serious evidence of Obama's sexism would never condone a bunch of women getting together without a male present .
What is this, cable? I read the Times to get away from this silliness.
Seriously? Who cares who he plays golf with? Who cares what kind of clothes he or his guest was wearing? You would think the New York Times would have more to report on with two wars going on and Obama's rating in the toilet.
I've always been a tomboy...sports fan, more comfortable hanging with the guys. I totally understand the bond amongst men, often centered around beer, sports, cigars (i.e. fun stuff!). It's a wonderful thing, just like the unique bond amongst women. The problem arises when work and important decisions are made during these all-male activities, whether intentional or not. I don't know what to say ladies...take up golf I suppose.
Unbelievable. This is "political correctness" without common sense. Dangerous.
I am ok if there are baby showers were just women are invited, I am ok if there are sports team only for girls, I am ok if there are bathrooms only for women, and I am ok if the President chooses to play sports with men. People can read way too much into things, and forget context, common sense, perspective and also a bit of humor.
Honestly, if the original story had not been on the front page, I would have thought it was a story from The Onion. I guess when the media is on a quest to find fault with someone, all areas are open.
Shouldn't Obama first decide about troops in Afghainstan before he takes time off to play golf?
my father made me take golf lessons during junior high school and high school - he said it was for my future. he was right... once the office found out that i played golf, i was invited to play with the "guys." it's true... a lot of work issues are discussed during a game - i don't think it's intentional but it happens.
Yeah, John Drew, just like it's a lot easier to be a right wing Christian if you don't apply all those silly principles to your own life-you know, the ones about feeding the poor, loving your neighbor, and all that nonesense.
Oh, puh-leeeze. Is there really nothing else to talk about?
Many times men are establishing relationships that further their careers when they get togther in activites such as these. Decisions are often made in such settings. If the all male club is sustained, women are put at a disadvantage. The people invited to such events are often the real inner circle. When women are excluded, they are put at a disadvantage.
If HRC had been elected president, how would men like it if she only invited women to all social functions and shut out men? My question is hypothetical since HRC did not win, but it would be interesting to see men's reactions to such a scenario.
For all of Obama's talk, I do think he harbors sexist attitudes.

In response to Liz: If you are playing golf with people who are talking about work you aren't really playing golf.
First of all, obviously the president and his staff seems to have realized that this is a problem. Why else, all of a sudden, Melody Barnes is playing golf with him?
Second, to all those people dismissing this story: it's not "just" a basketball game. Things like these might seem harmless on the surface, but can actually have a deleterious effect on women's upward movement in the workplace. It is exactly these kinds of informal, male networks that can give men the edge, when it comes time for performance reviews, bonuses, allocation of juicy assignments, promotions, etc.
This doesn't mean that men aren't allowed to play basketball anymore. It just means that corporate bosses need to be a lot of more sensitive about organizing after-work activities that are inclusive of everyone. If that means that you don't play basketball with your office, but with an external community league, so be it. It won't be the end of the world. Being an egalitarian leader means having to make some sacrifices.
We women who golf learned a long time ago that it helps solidify relationships, make men more comfortable off the course, and allows boht players to witness outside the office what we should already know from inside the office - namely, how people behave when things go your way - and when they don't. Is it with honesty, humility, humor, and strength? It's a great litmus test for any relationship, professional and personal. Good for President Obama for opening up the game.
Every moment of the president's day is scheduled. An opportunity to spend time with him is precious to anyone who wants to have an influence on public policy. That's why some people spend tens - if not hundreds - of thousands of dollars to play a round with him. This is Politics 101.
Every nay-saying commenter here acknowledged that these games help the president bond with his staff and other advisors. Bonding with your boss is important. Women deserve an equal opportunity to do this.
If you don't think its a problem that, after 22 golf games and numerous basketball games, NO women - until now - were given an equal opportunity to get face time and bond with the president, you are both a sexist and an ignoramus.
And don't fool yourselves - if a president Hillary Clinton organized 22+ golf games and a dozen bridge games and didn't invite any male staff, you male reporters and pundits and commenters would be shrieking with outrage. You'd be crying about man-hating and calling for her impeachment. Admit it.
We do NOT need a White House modeled on Mad Men. If that's the change B. has in mind, he can stuff it.

Spring is here, and it's the perfect time to discover fresh feelings of adventure.
If you are willing to explore your need for speed, then RepoKar is the right place for you! RepoKar is a fun, high-octane online Public Auto Auction where you're always one bid away from bringing home a car that fits your lifestyle.
RepoKar connects car dealers, government agencies, financial institutions, and car owners to buyers across the nation. As a buyer, you have the chance to win your dream car, and we'll help ensure it's delivered to your door.
If you need to buy a new car, browse through RepoKar's wide selection of pre-loved vehicles and experience a new way to shop.
The automotive industry is living in the golden age of fast-paced innovations, and consumers no longer want a car that takes them from point A to point B. They want an experience filled with the comfort and convenience technology provides. That's why RepoKar is offering new innovative ways of car buying.
Be confident when choosing your next 4-wheel drive because this season anything is possible.
Spring is the time to relax! Have an unforgettable trip in your new car!
Be the first to find the best car deals because buying on RepoKar means getting reliable cars at lower prices!
Want to learn more about automobiles? Head on over to our blog page. Read all the latest trends and other car-related news. You can also subscribe to our newsletter!
